strategy: Hit with Storm (+40%); she resists Myth 70%.
She's low health (3,620) — blade up over a round or two and end her in one big single hit; multi-hit spells make her counter-attack for each hit.
Damaging her makes her Mythblade herself and natural-attack you, but DoT ticks don't trigger it — a DoT or a stun (she's stunable) avoids the counter.
Watch the Storm Prism trigger — if she prisms herself your Storm converts to resisted Myth, so consider stunning or hitting through it.
Below 50% health she casts Time of Legend and Myth Traps a wizard every round, so burst her from above 50% straight to dead.
cheatNotes: Damage her and she Mythblades herself then natural-attacks you — end her in one hit or stun her first.
That extra Mythblade stacks with her normal one, so she hits harder each time.
A multi-hit spell makes her counter once per hit — use a single big hit instead.
DoT ticks don't trigger the counter — a DoT chips her safely.
Her counter natural attack can still be Dispelled.
On certain triggers she Storm Prisms herself and Tritons a wizard — the prism turns your Storm into resisted Myth.
Defeating a Lanyon Scratcher triggers it on whoever killed it.
Killing a Scratcher with a DoT won't trigger it.
Her Triton cheat can be Dispelled even though it isn't cast.
After her natural attack, if you still have a shield or trap she Pierces you.
If you still have a shield/trap/charm or a global is active and her last natural triggered Pierce, she Disarms you.
The round after she drops to 50% health she casts a +35% Time of Legend.
Every round below 50% she Myth Traps a random wizard — burst her past 50% to dead.
